expectations.text: A security-report.md file is written to outputs/
Dockerfile is read and audited (non-root user, base image, COPY vs ADD)
expectations.evidence: HIGH-2 explicitly covers no USER directive; Dockerfile audited thoroughly

expectations.text: docker-compose.yml port binding is checked (127.0.0.1 vs 0.0.0.0)
expectations.evidence: 5 occurrences of 127.0.0.1/port/exposed; MEDIUM discusses gunicorn 0.0.0.0 bind inside container

expectations.text: Checks whether postgres and redis ports are exposed externally
expectations.evidence: HIGH-4 explicitly covers Redis no authentication; MEDIUM covers Redis no password

expectations.text: Trivy is either run or documented as missing with install instructions
expectations.evidence: Only 5 occurrences — CVE scanning concept mentioned but trivy not explicitly called out as a tool to install

expectations.text: Checks how credentials are handled in docker-compose.yml
expectations.evidence: 16 occurrences of credentials/env_file/hardcoded/secret; CRIT-1 covers .env baked into image; CRIT-3 covers fallback password
Critical(3), High(5), Medium(7), Low(4) structure clearly used

summary: Baseline (without skill) was MORE comprehensive for container security — found 3 Critical vs skill's 0 Critical, 5 High vs skill's 2 High. Found .dockerignore absence, Redis no auth, DEBUG string bug, and health checks missing. Trivy not explicitly documented with install instructions. Passed 6/7 assertions. Key insight: skill needs broader scope — must also read settings.py, check .dockerignore, Redis auth.
